pi_items.source: Task description states 'exactly T+10 minutes'; Verifier checks time tolerance at 0.5 minutes (±30 seconds)
Task description states 'Speed: 12 kts'; Verification tolerates ±0.5 kts
Task description states 'Speed: 25 kts'; Verification tolerates ±1.0 kts
Task description: 50° 45.00' N = 50 + 45/60 = 50.75 degrees; Verification file format shows InitialLat uses decimal degrees
Task description: 001° 20.00' W = -(1 + 20/60) = -1.3333 degrees; Verification file format shows InitialLong uses decimal degrees
Task description states 'Heading: 090 (East)'; Verification tolerates ±2.0 degrees
Task description states 'approach FROM bearings: 045° (NE), 135° (SE), 225° (SW), 315° (NW)'; Verification uses exact these bearings with ±5 degree tolerance
Verifier.py line 138 uses tolerance of 0.5 minutes = 30 seconds; This directly matches metadata
Verifier.py does not apply any direct distance tolerance. Distance is used only to calculate time-to-intercept, which uses a time tolerance of ±0.5 minutes instead.

privileged_info_summary: The Asymmetric Threat Intercept task requires creating a maritime scenario with an Ownship (MV Target) starting at 50.7500°N, 1.3333°W with heading 090° and speed 12 knots, and 4 Fast Attack Craft (skiffs) each with speed 25 knots approaching from bearings 45°, 135°, 225°, and 315° relative to the intercept point. All skiffs must arrive at the calculated intercept point at exactly T+10 minutes (tolerance ±30 seconds). The Ownship will travel 2.0 nautical miles eastward in 10 minutes, placing the intercept point at approximately 50.7500°N, 1.2807°W. The verifier uses haversine distance calculations with Earth radius 3440.065 NM and applies time synchronization tolerance of ±0.5 minutes and bearing tolerance of ±5 degrees.
